tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of impaired renal function (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), hypoglycemia, and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic kidney disease with poor glycemic control or malnutrition. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria) further support renal dysfunction and possible hepatic involvement. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
